Page.Width: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualWidth, FallbackValue=700}
Page.Height: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualHeight, FallbackValue=700}

Run.Text: Sonar detection is greatly influenced by several factors. The most important is your ship's speed.

However, environmental factors also affect sonar detection. Higher sea states generate more ambient noise, making passive detection more difficult. Rain has the same effect by creating noise on the surface. Note that snow does not affect passive detection!

In higher sea states, ASW becomes more and more difficult. Sea state is influenced by the current Wind Force. You can see the current conditions by opening the Event Log at bottom right, then checking the Environment tab to see the current Wind Force and ambient ocean noise level. Higher Wind Force equates to more waves on the surface, equalling higher noise level. This means passive detection by all platforms (including submarines) is more difficult.
